name: Durga Puja
description: The grandest festival of Bengal where streets transform into open-air art galleries with massive pandals.

name: Holi
description: The festival of colors celebrated with unmatched fervor in the birthplace of Lord Krishna.

name: Pushkar Camel Fair
description: One of the world's largest camel fairs with desert sunsets, folk music and thousands of camels.

name: Hornbill Festival
description: All 16 Naga tribes come together in a celebration of warrior culture, traditional dances and indigenous food.

name: Ziro Festival of Music
description: India's most unique outdoor music festival set in the stunning Ziro valley amid Apatani tribal culture.

name: Rann Utsav
description: A festival of white desert, full moon nights and Gujarati folk culture in the vast salt flats.

name: Thrissur Pooram
description: Decorated elephants, percussion orchestras and fireworks at dawn in the mother of all temple festivals.

name: Chapchar Kut
description: Mizoram's biggest spring festival with traditional bamboo dances and colorful Mizo costumes.

name: Kumbh Mela
description: The world's largest human gathering where millions of pilgrims take a holy dip at the Sangam.

name: Hampi Utsav
description: A three-day celebration of music, dance and puppet shows amid the ancient ruins of the Vijayanagara Empire.

name: Losar Festival
description: Tibetan New Year celebrated with masked dances, butter sculptures and monastery rituals in the Himalayas.

name: Diwali
description: The festival of lights is most magical in Varanasi where the ghats glow with thousands of earthen lamps.

name: Tawang Festival
description: Monpa tribal festival near the famous Tawang Monastery with traditional dances and indigenous crafts.

name: Bihu
description: Assam's most important harvest festival with energetic Bihu dances and traditional music.

name: Pongal
description: Tamil harvest festival celebrated with colorful kolams, sugarcane and the famous Jallikattu bull taming.

name: Cherry Blossom Festival
description: Hanami picnicking under blooming cherry trees transforms Tokyo's parks into pink wonderlands.

name: Gion Matsuri
description: Ancient floats parade through Kyoto's old streets in Japan's most famous month-long festival.

name: Sapporo Snow Festival
description: Massive snow and ice sculptures transform Sapporo into a frozen wonderland attracting 2 million visitors.

name: Awa Odori Dance Festival
description: Japan's largest dance festival where 1.3 million spectators watch performers in traditional Awa dance.

name: Kanamara Matsuri
description: Unique Japanese fertility festival featuring elaborate phallus-shaped mikoshi carried through the streets.

name: Dashain Festival
description: Nepal's biggest festival where kites fill the sky, families reunite and the whole country transforms.

name: Indra Jatra
description: Ancient Newari festival with living goddess Kumari paraded through Kathmandu's old city streets.

name: Songkran Water Festival
description: Thai New Year celebrated with the world's biggest water fight lasting three days.

name: Yi Peng Lantern Festival
description: Thousands of paper lanterns released simultaneously into the night sky create one of earth's most breathtaking sights.

name: Phi Ta Khon Ghost Festival
description: Remote Thai village festival where locals wear elaborate ghost masks and costumes in a joyful procession.

name: Oktoberfest
description: The world's largest beer festival with six million visitors, Bavarian culture and massive tents.

name: Carnival of Venice
description: The most mysterious carnival in the world with ornate masks, Renaissance costumes and gondolas on misty canals.

name: La Tomatina
description: The world's biggest tomato fight where 20,000 people hurl 150,000 tomatoes at each other.

name: Rio Carnival
description: The greatest show on earth with samba schools of 5000 performers and 2 million people on the streets daily.

name: Day of the Dead
description: Mexico's most moving celebration with marigold altars, candlelit cemetery vigils and colorful processions.

name: Burning Man
description: A radical experiment in temporary community art and self-expression in the Nevada desert.

name: Mardi Gras
description: New Orleans' legendary carnival with jazz parades, elaborate floats and beads thrown from balconies.

name: Timkat Festival
description: Ethiopian Orthodox Epiphany celebration with priests carrying the Ark of the Covenant replica through crowds.

name: Mombasa Carnival
description: East Africa's biggest street carnival with colorful floats, traditional dances and Swahili culture.

name: Thaipusam
description: Hindu devotees carry elaborately decorated kavadis up 272 steps to the Batu Caves temple.

name: Vesak Festival
description: The most sacred Buddhist holiday illuminated by millions of lanterns and pandals across the island.

name: Nowruz
description: Persian New Year marking the spring equinox celebrated by 300 million people across Central Asia.

name: Glastonbury Festival
description: The world's most famous music festival held on a Somerset farm attracting 200,000 people.

name: Edinburgh Festival Fringe
description: The world's largest arts festival with 3000 shows across 300 venues in Edinburgh's historic streets.

name: Notting Hill Carnival
description: Europe's biggest street festival celebrating Caribbean culture with steel bands and elaborate costumes.

name: Carnaval de Barranquilla
description: UNESCO-listed carnival second only to Rio with cumbia, mapalé and vallenato music filling the streets.

name: Inti Raymi
description: Inca Festival of the Sun reenacted at Sacsayhuaman fortress above Cusco with 500 performers.

name: Carnival of Oruro
description: UNESCO-listed Andean carnival with diablada devil dance and 28,000 dancers in the altiplano.

name: Harbin Ice Festival
description: Largest ice and snow sculpture festival in the world with illuminated ice castles and sculptures.

name: Lantern Festival
description: Thousands of sky lanterns released into the night sky over Pingxi creating a magical river of light.

name: Naadam Festival
description: Mongolia's national festival of the three manly games: archery, wrestling and horse racing on the steppe.

name: Boryeong Mud Festival
description: Quirky Korean festival where 2 million people revel in mud slides, mud wrestling and mud cosmetics.

name: Diwali
description: The Pink City explodes with fireworks and oil lamps making Jaipur one of the best Diwali destinations.

name: Gangaur Festival
description: Rajasthani women celebrate Goddess Gauri with colorful processions and elaborate rituals for 18 days.

name: Hemis Festival
description: Ladakh's largest monastic festival with masked Cham dances performed by monks at Hemis Monastery.

name: Taj Mahotsav
description: Ten-day festival of arts, crafts, culture and cuisine near the Taj Mahal showcasing Mughal heritage.

name: Balloon Festival
description: Thousands of hot air balloons and fire balloons illuminate the night sky over Inle Lake region.

name: Bun Bang Fai Rocket Festival
description: Ancient festival where villages launch homemade rockets to encourage rainfall for the rice harvest.

name: Carnival of Binche
description: UNESCO-listed carnival where Gilles in ostrich-feather hats throw blood oranges at crowds.

name: Up Helly Aa
description: Europe's largest fire festival where 1000 Vikings march through Shetland before torching a longship.

name: Onam
description: Kerala's harvest festival celebrated with snake boat races, floral carpets and elaborate feasts.

name: International Kite Festival
description: Skies over Ahmedabad fill with thousands of colorful kites during Makar Sankranti celebration.

name: Desert Festival
description: Camel races, folk performances and turban-tying competitions against the backdrop of golden sand dunes.

name: Wachuma Ceremony
description: Ancient Andean winter solstice ceremony at sacred sites connecting with Pachamama the earth mother.

name: Cape Town Jazz Festival
description: Africa's biggest jazz festival with 40 local and international acts performing across multiple stages.

name: Fes Festival of World Sacred Music
description: Sufi music, sacred chants and world music performances in the ancient medina of Fes.

name: Gnaoua Music Festival
description: Hypnotic Gnaoua trance music fuses with jazz and world music in the windswept Atlantic coast city.

name: Loy Krathong
description: Ancient Sukhothai floats thousands of lotus-shaped krathong on its historic ponds in magical candlelight.

name: Tiji Festival
description: Three-day festival in the forbidden kingdom of Upper Mustang with Thangka paintings and Cham dances.

name: Holi in Vrindavan
description: The most sacred Holi celebration at the birthplace of Lord Krishna with flower petals and natural colors.

name: Lathmar Holi
description: Women beat men with bamboo sticks in this unique pre-Holi tradition in the village of Barsana.

name: Teej Festival
description: Women in red saris sing and dance at Pashupatinath temple praying for their husbands' longevity.

name: Tsechu Festival
description: Bhutan's most celebrated festival with sacred Cham dances and the unfurling of a giant thangka painting.

name: Vaisakhi
description: Sikh harvest festival celebrated with Bhangra dancing and prayer at the Golden Temple.

name: Fiesta de San Fermin
description: Nine days of celebrations including the world-famous running of the bulls through Pamplona's old streets.

name: Diwali in Goa
description: Goa's unique Diwali tradition burns giant effigies of demon Narakasura at dawn across the state.

name: Winter Carnival
description: North America's largest winter festival with ice sculptures, snow baths and the famous ice palace.

name: Carnaval de Cadiz
description: Spain's wittiest carnival where satirical singing groups called chirigotas mock politicians in the streets.

name: Beltane Fire Festival
description: Ancient Celtic fire festival revived on Calton Hill with drummers, fire performers and the May Queen.

name: Hadaka Matsuri Naked Festival
description: 9000 men in white loincloths crowd Saidaiji temple fighting over sacred lucky sticks in the winter cold.

name: Phuket Vegetarian Festival
description: Extraordinary Chinese-Thai festival where devotees pierce their bodies with swords to demonstrate spiritual power.

name: Esala Perahera
description: Ancient Buddhist pageant with 100 elaborately decorated elephants parading through Kandy's streets at night.

name: Shivaratri at Pashupatinath
description: Millions of Shaivite pilgrims and sadhus converge on Pashupatinath temple for the great night of Shiva.

name: Sonepur Cattle Fair
description: Asia's largest cattle fair held at the confluence of Ganges and Gandak rivers with elephants and folk arts.

name: Nagaur Cattle Fair
description: Rajasthan's colorful cattle fair with camel races, puppet shows and traditional Rajasthani folk music.

name: Goa Carnival
description: Portuguese-influenced carnival with floats, music and dancing through Goa's colourful streets.

name: Wangala Festival
description: Garo tribe's harvest festival with 100 drummers and traditional dances honoring the Sun God Saljong.

name: Sangai Festival
description: Manipur's biggest festival showcasing the endangered Sangai deer alongside indigenous arts and martial arts.

name: Nuakhai
description: Odisha's agricultural festival where new rice is offered to goddess Samaleswari before the harvest begins.

name: Ambubachi Mela
description: Annual Tantric festival at Kamakhya temple where sadhus from across India gather for four sacred days.

name: Thrissur Pooram
description: Two groups of caparisoned elephants face each other in a percussion battle that lasts all night.

name: Kerala Boat Race
description: Nehru Trophy snake boat race with 100-oared boats racing on Punnamada Lake to thunderous applause.

name: Mysuru Dasara
description: Royal celebrations with illuminated Mysore Palace, elephant procession and a massive torch procession.

name: Oktoberfest Replica
description: The world's third biggest Oktoberfest held in Brazil's German immigrant city with genuine Bavarian culture.

name: Carnaval de Montevideo
description: The world's longest carnival lasting the entire month of February with candombe drums and murga groups.

name: Navroz in Samarkand
description: Persian New Year celebrated with sumalak wheat pudding, music and games in the ancient Silk Road city.

name: Festa Junina
description: Brazil's biggest June festival with forró music, quadrilha dancing and mock rural weddings.
